preview_text:
The model favors France at 55%, with Norway given a 17% chance of victory. That gap reflects a substantial asymmetry: France enters as the clear favorite, while Norway faces long odds to win on a neutral ground.

The draw probability of 28% sits comfortably between the win chances, suggesting the match could plausibly end level. The 37-percentage-point spread between France's win probability and Norway's indicates a competitive imbalance rather than a heavily one-sided affair—draws remain a realistic outcome, and Norway retains a meaningful path to points despite the odds.

Model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The sportsbook consensus places France at 52% (3 points below the model), Norway at 22% (5 points above), and draws at 26% (2 points below). Polymarket prices track similarly. These small gaps suggest broad agreement on the expected shape of the match, though slight daylight remains on France's win probability.
